[[ja:太田茶臼山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Oda Chausuyama Kofun | image = [[File:Oda Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (front mound on the left, rear mound in the back right) | Belongs to = {{ill|Mishima Kofun Cluster|ja|三島古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Oda 3-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|50|40.50|N|135|34|42.36|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | Dimensions = Total length: 226 meters; height (front mound): 19.8 meters | Construction era = Mid-5th century | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}} | Excavated items = Cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, and more | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Mishima Aino Mausoleum" (三嶋藍野陵) | Special notes = 21st largest tumulus in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Rankings of Kofun Sizes (National Version)] (Sakai City Website, updated May 13, 2018).
Similar in shape to {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}} (Fujidera, Osaka Prefecture) | Map = Japan Osaka | Label = Oda Chausuyama
Tumulus | Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-south-southeast | Label position = top }} '''Oda Chausuyama Kofun''' (太田茶臼山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Oda. It is the largest tumulus in northern Osaka and ranks as the 21st largest in Japan. Though the actual interred individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as "Mishima Aino Mausoleum," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}}. == Overview == The tumulus was constructed on the {{ill|Tonda Plateau|ja|富田台地}} near the right bank of the {{ill|Yodo River|ja|淀川}}. Current management falls under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, which recognizes it as Emperor Keitai's mausoleum. Excavations outside the surrounding moat have been conducted by the Osaka Prefectural and Ibaraki City Boards of Education in 1972 and 1988. The Imperial Household Agency's Sho-Ryo Division also carried out a survey at the mound's base in 2002. The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented south. It measures 226 meters in total length, making it the largest in northern Osaka. Its moat and embankments encircle the mound in a shield shape, with figurative and cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} excavated from its surroundings. Production of these haniwa has been linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Production Site|ja|史跡新池ハニワ工場公園}} in Takatsuki.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第7巻 史料編 考古|2014|pp=293-297}} Other small tombs, believed to be satellite mounds, are located nearby. == Burial Individual == The actual burial individual is unknown, but the Imperial Household Agency attributes the tumulus to Emperor Keitai. Some scholars argue that {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳}} (Takatsuki, Osaka Prefecture) better matches historical records and Emperor Keitai's era.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第1巻 通史|2012|pp=381-389}} == Excavated Items == Artifacts such as cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, and roof tiles have been unearthed. These findings are stored and displayed at the {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館}}. == Cultural Importance == The surrounding moat is designated as part of a national historic site, and the tumulus holds significant cultural value as a testament to the burial practices and social structures of 5th-century Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/026/index.html Mishima Aino Mausoleum] - Imperial Household Agency * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Rankings of Kofun Sizes (Sakai City)] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
[[ja:太田茶臼山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Oda Chausuyama Kofun | image = [[File:Oda Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (front mound on the left, rear mound in the back right) | Belongs to = {{ill|Mishima Kofun Cluster|ja|三島古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Oda 3-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|50|40.50|N|135|34|42.36|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | Dimensions = Total length: 226 meters; height (front mound): 19.8 meters | Construction era = Mid-5th century | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}} | Excavated items = Cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, and more | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Mishima Aino Mausoleum" (三嶋藍野陵) | Special notes = 21st largest tumulus in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Rankings of Kofun Sizes (National Version)] (Sakai City Website, updated May 13, 2018).
Similar in shape to {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}} (Fujidera, Osaka Prefecture) | Map = Japan Osaka | Label = Oda Chausuyama
Tumulus | Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-south-southeast | Label position = top }} '''Oda Chausuyama Kofun''' (太田茶臼山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Oda. It is the largest tumulus in northern Osaka and ranks as the 21st largest in Japan. Though the actual interred individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as "Mishima Aino Mausoleum," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}}. == Overview == The tumulus was constructed on the {{ill|Tonda Plateau|ja|富田台地}} near the right bank of the {{ill|Yodo River|ja|淀川}}. Current management falls under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, which recognizes it as Emperor Keitai's mausoleum. Excavations outside the surrounding moat have been conducted by the Osaka Prefectural and Ibaraki City Boards of Education in 1972 and 1988. The Imperial Household Agency's Sho-Ryo Division also carried out a survey at the mound's base in 2002. The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented south. It measures 226 meters in total length, making it the largest in northern Osaka. Its moat and embankments encircle the mound in a shield shape, with figurative and cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} excavated from its surroundings. Production of these haniwa has been linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Production Site|ja|史跡新池ハニワ工場公園}} in Takatsuki.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第7巻 史料編 考古|2014|pp=293-297}} Other small tombs, believed to be satellite mounds, are located nearby. == Burial Individual == The actual burial individual is unknown, but the Imperial Household Agency attributes the tumulus to Emperor Keitai. Some scholars argue that {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳}} (Takatsuki, Osaka Prefecture) better matches historical records and Emperor Keitai's era.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第1巻 通史|2012|pp=381-389}} == Excavated Items == Artifacts such as cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, and roof tiles have been unearthed. These findings are stored and displayed at the {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館}}. == Cultural Importance == The surrounding moat is designated as part of a national historic site, and the tumulus holds significant cultural value as a testament to the burial practices and social structures of 5th-century Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/026/index.html Mishima Aino Mausoleum] - Imperial Household Agency * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Rankings of Kofun Sizes (Sakai City)] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] Similar in shape to {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}} (Fujidera, Osaka Prefecture) | Map = Japan Osaka | Label = Oda Chausuyama
Tumulus | Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-south-southeast | Label position = top }} '''Oda Chausuyama Kofun''' (太田茶臼山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Oda. It is the largest tumulus in northern Osaka and ranks as the 21st largest in Japan. Though the actual interred individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as "Mishima Aino Mausoleum," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}}. == Overview == The tumulus was constructed on the {{ill|Tonda Plateau|ja|富田台地}} near the right bank of the {{ill|Yodo River|ja|淀川}}. Current management falls under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, which recognizes it as Emperor Keitai's mausoleum. Excavations outside the surrounding moat have been conducted by the Osaka Prefectural and Ibaraki City Boards of Education in 1972 and 1988. The Imperial Household Agency's Sho-Ryo Division also carried out a survey at the mound's base in 2002. The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented south. It measures 226 meters in total length, making it the largest in northern Osaka. Its moat and embankments encircle the mound in a shield shape, with figurative and cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} excavated from its surroundings. Production of these haniwa has been linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Production Site|ja|史跡新池ハニワ工場公園}} in Takatsuki.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第7巻 史料編 考古|2014|pp=293-297}} Other small tombs, believed to be satellite mounds, are located nearby. == Burial Individual == The actual burial individual is unknown, but the Imperial Household Agency attributes the tumulus to Emperor Keitai. Some scholars argue that {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳}} (Takatsuki, Osaka Prefecture) better matches historical records and Emperor Keitai's era.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第1巻 通史|2012|pp=381-389}} == Excavated Items == Artifacts such as cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, and roof tiles have been unearthed. These findings are stored and displayed at the {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館}}. == Cultural Importance == The surrounding moat is designated as part of a national historic site, and the tumulus holds significant cultural value as a testament to the burial practices and social structures of 5th-century Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/026/index.html Mishima Aino Mausoleum] - Imperial Household Agency * Production of these haniwa has been linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Production Site|ja|史跡新池ハニワ工場公園}} in Takatsuki.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第7巻 史料編 考古|2014|pp=293-297}} Other small tombs, believed to be satellite mounds, are located nearby. == Burial Individual == The actual burial individual is unknown, but the Imperial Household Agency attributes the tumulus to Emperor Keitai. Some scholars argue that {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳}} (Takatsuki, Osaka Prefecture) better matches historical records and Emperor Keitai's era.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第1巻 通史|2012|pp=381-389}} == Excavated Items == Artifacts such as cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, and roof tiles have been unearthed. These findings are stored and displayed at the {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館}}. == Cultural Importance == The surrounding moat is designated as part of a national historic site, and the tumulus holds significant cultural value as a testament to the burial practices and social structures of 5th-century Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/026/index.html Mishima Aino Mausoleum] - Imperial Household Agency * Tumulus | Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-south-southeast | Label position = top }} '''Oda Chausuyama Kofun''' (太田茶臼山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Oda. It is the largest tumulus in northern Osaka and ranks as the 21st largest in Japan. Though the actual interred individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as "Mishima Aino Mausoleum," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}}. == Overview == The tumulus was constructed on the {{ill|Tonda Plateau|ja|富田台地}} near the right bank of the {{ill|Yodo River|ja|淀川}}. Current management falls under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, which recognizes it as Emperor Keitai's mausoleum. Excavations outside the surrounding moat have been conducted by the Osaka Prefectural and Ibaraki City Boards of Education in 1972 and 1988. The Imperial Household Agency's Sho-Ryo Division also carried out a survey at the mound's base in 2002. The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented south. It measures 226 meters in total length, making it the largest in northern Osaka. Its moat and embankments encircle the mound in a shield shape, with figurative and cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} excavated from its surroundings. Production of these haniwa has been linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Production Site|ja|史跡新池ハニワ工場公園}} in Takatsuki.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第7巻 史料編 考古|2014|pp=293-297}} Other small tombs, believed to be satellite mounds, are located nearby. == Burial Individual == The actual burial individual is unknown, but the Imperial Household Agency attributes the tumulus to Emperor Keitai. Some scholars argue that {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳}} (Takatsuki, Osaka Prefecture) better matches historical records and Emperor Keitai's era.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第1巻 通史|2012|pp=381-389}} == Excavated Items == Artifacts such as cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, and roof tiles have been unearthed. These findings are stored and displayed at the {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館}}. == Cultural Importance == The surrounding moat is designated as part of a national historic site, and the tumulus holds significant cultural value as a testament to the burial practices and social structures of 5th-century Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Ibaraki revision.text:
[[ja:太田茶臼山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Oda Chausuyama Kofun | image = [[File:Oda Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (front mound on the left, rear mound in the back right) | Belongs to = {{ill|Mishima Kofun Cluster|ja|三島古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Oda 3-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|50|40.50|N|135|34|42.36|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Dimensions = Total length: 226 meters; height (front mound): 19.8 meters | Construction era = Mid-5th century | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}} | Excavated items = Cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, and more | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Mishima Aino Mausoleum" (三嶋藍野陵) | Special notes = 21st largest tumulus in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Rankings of Kofun Sizes (National Version)] (Sakai City Website, updated May 13, 2018).
Similar in shape to {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}} (Fujidera, Osaka Prefecture) | Map = Japan Osaka | Label = Oda Chausuyama
Tumulus | Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-south-southeast | Label position = top }} '''Oda Chausuyama Kofun''' (太田茶臼山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Oda. It is the largest tumulus in northern Osaka and ranks as the 21st largest in Japan. Though the actual interred individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as "Mishima Aino Mausoleum," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}}. == Overview == The tumulus was constructed on the {{ill|Tonda Plateau|ja|富田台地}} near the right bank of the {{ill|Yodo River|ja|淀川}}. Current management falls under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, which recognizes it as Emperor Keitai's mausoleum. Excavations outside the surrounding moat have been conducted by the Osaka Prefectural and Ibaraki City Boards of Education in 1972 and 1988. The Imperial Household Agency's Sho-Ryo Division also carried out a survey at the mound's base in 2002. The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented south. It measures 226 meters in total length, making it the largest in northern Osaka. Its moat and embankments encircle the mound in a shield shape, with figurative and cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} excavated from its surroundings. Production of these haniwa has been linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Production Site|ja|史跡新池ハニワ工場公園}} in Takatsuki.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第7巻 史料編 考古|2014|pp=293-297}} Other small tombs, believed to be satellite mounds, are located nearby. == Burial Individual == The actual burial individual is unknown, but the Imperial Household Agency attributes the tumulus to Emperor Keitai. Some scholars argue that {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳}} (Takatsuki, Osaka Prefecture) better matches historical records and Emperor Keitai's era.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第1巻 通史|2012|pp=381-389}} == Excavated Items == Artifacts such as cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, and roof tiles have been unearthed. These findings are stored and displayed at the {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館}}. == Cultural Importance == The surrounding moat is designated as part of a national historic site, and the tumulus holds significant cultural value as a testament to the burial practices and social structures of 5th-century Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/026/index.html Mishima Aino Mausoleum] - Imperial Household Agency * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Rankings of Kofun Sizes (Sakai City)] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] It is the largest tumulus in northern Osaka and ranks as the 21st largest in Japan. Though the actual interred individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as "Mishima Aino Mausoleum," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}}. == Overview == The tumulus was constructed on the {{ill|Tonda Plateau|ja|富田台地}} near the right bank of the {{ill|Yodo River|ja|淀川}}. Current management falls under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, which recognizes it as Emperor Keitai's mausoleum. Excavations outside the surrounding moat have been conducted by the Osaka Prefectural and Ibaraki City Boards of Education in 1972 and 1988. The Imperial Household Agency's Sho-Ryo Division also carried out a survey at the mound's base in 2002. The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented south. It measures 226 meters in total length, making it the largest in northern Osaka. Its moat and embankments encircle the mound in a shield shape, with figurative and cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} excavated from its surroundings. Production of these haniwa has been linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Production Site|ja|史跡新池ハニワ工場公園}} in Takatsuki.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第7巻 史料編 考古|2014|pp=293-297}} Other small tombs, believed to be satellite mounds, are located nearby. == Burial Individual == The actual burial individual is unknown, but the Imperial Household Agency attributes the tumulus to Emperor Keitai. Some scholars argue that {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳}} (Takatsuki, Osaka Prefecture) better matches historical records and Emperor Keitai's era.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第1巻 通史|2012|pp=381-389}} == Excavated Items == Artifacts such as cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, and roof tiles have been unearthed. These findings are stored and displayed at the {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館}}. == Cultural Importance == The surrounding moat is designated as part of a national historic site, and the tumulus holds significant cultural value as a testament to the burial practices and social structures of 5th-century Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/026/index.html Mishima Aino Mausoleum] - Imperial Household Agency * [[ja:太田茶臼山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Oda Chausuyama Kofun | image = [[File:Oda Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (front mound on the left, rear mound in the back right) | Belongs to = {{ill|Mishima Kofun Cluster|ja|三島古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Oda 3-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|50|40.50|N|135|34|42.36|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Dimensions = Total length: 226 meters; height (front mound): 19.8 meters | Construction era = Mid-5th century | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}} | Excavated items = Cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, and more | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Mishima Aino Mausoleum" (三嶋藍野陵) | Special notes = 21st largest tumulus in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Rankings of Kofun Sizes (National Version)] (Sakai City Website, updated May 13, 2018).
Similar in shape to {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}} (Fujidera, Osaka Prefecture) | Map = Japan Osaka | Label = Oda Chausuyama
Tumulus | Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-south-southeast | Label position = top }} '''Oda Chausuyama Kofun''' (太田茶臼山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Oda. It is the largest tumulus in northern Osaka and ranks as the 21st largest in Japan. Though the actual interred individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as "Mishima Aino Mausoleum," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}}. == Overview == The tumulus was constructed on the {{ill|Tonda Plateau|ja|富田台地}} near the right bank of the {{ill|Yodo River|ja|淀川}}. Current management falls under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, which recognizes it as Emperor Keitai's mausoleum. Excavations outside the surrounding moat have been conducted by the Osaka Prefectural and Ibaraki City Boards of Education in 1972 and 1988. The Imperial Household Agency's Sho-Ryo Division also carried out a survey at the mound's base in 2002. The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented south. It measures 226 meters in total length, making it the largest in northern Osaka. Its moat and embankments encircle the mound in a shield shape, with figurative and cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} excavated from its surroundings. Production of these haniwa has been linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Production Site|ja|史跡新池ハニワ工場公園}} in Takatsuki.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第7巻 史料編 考古|2014|pp=293-297}} Other small tombs, believed to be satellite mounds, are located nearby. == Burial Individual == The actual burial individual is unknown, but the Imperial Household Agency attributes the tumulus to Emperor Keitai. Some scholars argue that {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳}} (Takatsuki, Osaka Prefecture) better matches historical records and Emperor Keitai's era.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第1巻 通史|2012|pp=381-389}} == Excavated Items == Artifacts such as cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, and roof tiles have been unearthed. These findings are stored and displayed at the {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館}}. == Cultural Importance == The surrounding moat is designated as part of a national historic site, and the tumulus holds significant cultural value as a testament to the burial practices and social structures of 5th-century Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/026/index.html Mishima Aino Mausoleum] - Imperial Household Agency * Tumulus (front mound on the left, rear mound in the back right) | Belongs to = {{ill|Mishima Kofun Cluster|ja|三島古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Oda 3-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|50|40.50|N|135|34|42.36|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Dimensions = Total length: 226 meters; height (front mound): 19.8 meters | Construction era = Mid-5th century | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}} | Excavated items = Cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, and more | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Mishima Aino Mausoleum" (三嶋藍野陵) | Special notes = 21st largest tumulus in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Rankings of Kofun Sizes (National Version)] (Sakai City Website, updated May 13, 2018).
Similar in shape to {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}} (Fujidera, Osaka Prefecture) | Map = Japan Osaka | Label = Oda Chausuyama
Tumulus | Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-south-southeast | Label position = top }} '''Oda Chausuyama Kofun''' (太田茶臼山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Oda. It is the largest tumulus in northern Osaka and ranks as the 21st largest in Japan. Though the actual interred individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as "Mishima Aino Mausoleum," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}}. == Overview == The tumulus was constructed on the {{ill|Tonda Plateau|ja|富田台地}} near the right bank of the {{ill|Yodo River|ja|淀川}}. Current management falls under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, which recognizes it as Emperor Keitai's mausoleum. Excavations outside the surrounding moat have been conducted by the Osaka Prefectural and Ibaraki City Boards of Education in 1972 and 1988. The Imperial Household Agency's Sho-Ryo Division also carried out a survey at the mound's base in 2002. The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented south. It measures 226 meters in total length, making it the largest in northern Osaka. Its moat and embankments encircle the mound in a shield shape, with figurative and cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} excavated from its surroundings. Production of these haniwa has been linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Production Site|ja|史跡新池ハニワ工場公園}} in Takatsuki.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第7巻 史料編 考古|2014|pp=293-297}} Other small tombs, believed to be satellite mounds, are located nearby. == Burial Individual == The actual burial individual is unknown, but the Imperial Household Agency attributes the tumulus to Emperor Keitai. Some scholars argue that {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳}} (Takatsuki, Osaka Prefecture) better matches historical records and Emperor Keitai's era.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第1巻 通史|2012|pp=381-389}} == Excavated Items == Artifacts such as cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, and roof tiles have been unearthed. These findings are stored and displayed at the {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館}}. == Cultural Importance == The surrounding moat is designated as part of a national historic site, and the tumulus holds significant cultural value as a testament to the burial practices and social structures of 5th-century Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/026/index.html Mishima Aino Mausoleum] - Imperial Household Agency * [[ja:太田茶臼山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Oda Chausuyama Kofun | image = [[File:Oda Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (front mound on the left, rear mound in the back right) | Belongs to = {{ill|Mishima Kofun Cluster|ja|三島古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Oda 3-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|50|40.50|N|135|34|42.36|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Dimensions = Total length: 226 meters; height (front mound): 19.8 meters | Construction era = Mid-5th century | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}} | Excavated items = Cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, and more | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Mishima Aino Mausoleum" (三嶋藍野陵) | Special notes = 21st largest tumulus in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Rankings of Kofun Sizes (National Version)] (Sakai City Website, updated May 13, 2018).
Similar in shape to {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}} (Fujidera, Osaka Prefecture) | Map = Japan Osaka | Label = Oda Chausuyama
Kofun | Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-south-southeast | Label position = top }} '''Oda Chausuyama Kofun''' (太田茶臼山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Oda. It is the largest tumulus in northern Osaka and ranks as the 21st largest in Japan. Though the actual interred individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as "Mishima Aino Mausoleum," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}}. == Overview == The tumulus was constructed on the {{ill|Tonda Plateau|ja|富田台地}} near the right bank of the {{ill|Yodo River|ja|淀川}}. Current management falls under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, which recognizes it as Emperor Keitai's mausoleum. Excavations outside the surrounding moat have been conducted by the Osaka Prefectural and Ibaraki City Boards of Education in 1972 and 1988. The Imperial Household Agency's Sho-Ryo Division also carried out a survey at the mound's base in 2002. The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented south. It measures 226 meters in total length, making it the largest in northern Osaka. Its moat and embankments encircle the mound in a shield shape, with figurative and cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} excavated from its surroundings. Production of these haniwa has been linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Production Site|ja|史跡新池ハニワ工場公園}} in Takatsuki.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第7巻 史料編 考古|2014|pp=293-297}} Other small tombs, believed to be satellite mounds, are located nearby. == Burial Individual == The actual burial individual is unknown, but the Imperial Household Agency attributes the tumulus to Emperor Keitai. Some scholars argue that {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳}} (Takatsuki, Osaka Prefecture) better matches historical records and Emperor Keitai's era.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第1巻 通史|2012|pp=381-389}} == Excavated Items == Artifacts such as cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, and roof tiles have been unearthed. These findings are stored and displayed at the {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館}}. == Cultural Importance == The surrounding moat is designated as part of a national historic site, and the tumulus holds significant cultural value as a testament to the burial practices and social structures of 5th-century Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/026/index.html Mishima Aino Mausoleum] - Imperial Household Agency * Some scholars argue that {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳}} (Takatsuki, Osaka Prefecture) better matches historical records and Emperor Keitai's era.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第1巻 通史|2012|pp=381-389}} == Excavated Items == Artifacts such as cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, and roof tiles have been unearthed. These findings are stored and displayed at the {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館}}. == Cultural Importance == The surrounding moat is designated as part of a national historic site, and the [[ja:太田茶臼山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Oda Chausuyama Kofun | image = [[File:Oda Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (front mound on the left, rear mound in the back right) | Belongs to = {{ill|Mishima Kofun Cluster|ja|三島古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Oda 3-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|50|40.50|N|135|34|42.36|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Dimensions = Total length: 226 meters; height (front mound): 19.8 meters | Construction era = Mid-5th century | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}} | Excavated items = Cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, and more | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Mishima Aino Mausoleum" (三嶋藍野陵) | Special notes = 21st largest tumulus in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Rankings of Kofun Sizes (National Version)] (Sakai City Website, updated May 13, 2018).
Similar in shape to {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}} (Fujidera, Osaka Prefecture) | Map = Japan Osaka | Label = Oda Chausuyama
Kofun | Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-south-southeast | Label position = top }} '''Oda Chausuyama Kofun''' (太田茶臼山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Oda. It is the largest tumulus in northern Osaka and ranks as the 21st largest in Japan. Though the actual interred individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as "Mishima Aino Mausoleum," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}}. == Overview == The tumulus was constructed on the {{ill|Tonda Plateau|ja|富田台地}} near the right bank of the {{ill|Yodo River|ja|淀川}}. Current management falls under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, which recognizes it as Emperor Keitai's mausoleum. Excavations outside the surrounding moat have been conducted by the Osaka Prefectural and Ibaraki City Boards of Education in 1972 and 1988. The Imperial Household Agency's Sho-Ryo Division also carried out a survey at the mound's base in 2002. The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented south. It measures 226 meters in total length, making it the largest in northern Osaka. Its moat and embankments encircle the mound in a shield shape, with figurative and cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} excavated from its surroundings. Production of these haniwa has been linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Production Site|ja|史跡新池ハニワ工場公園}} in Takatsuki.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第7巻 史料編 考古|2014|pp=293-297}} Other small tombs, believed to be satellite mounds, are located nearby. == Burial Individual == The actual burial individual is unknown, but the Imperial Household Agency attributes the tumulus to Emperor Keitai. Some scholars argue that {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳}} (Takatsuki, Osaka Prefecture) better matches historical records and Emperor Keitai's era.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第1巻 通史|2012|pp=381-389}} == Excavated Items == Artifacts such as cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, and roof tiles have been unearthed. These findings are stored and displayed at the {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館}}. == Cultural Importance == The surrounding moat is designated as part of a national historic site, and the tumulus holds significant cultural value as a testament to the burial practices and social structures of 5th-century Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/026/index.html Mishima Aino Mausoleum] - Imperial Household Agency * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Rankings of Kofun Sizes (Sakai City)] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[ja:太田茶臼山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Oda Chausuyama Kofun | image = [[File:Oda Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (front mound on the left, rear mound in the back right) | Belongs to = {{ill|Mishima Kofun Cluster|ja|三島古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Oda 3-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|50|40.50|N|135|34|42.36|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Dimensions = Total length: 226 meters; height (front mound): 19.8 meters | Construction era = Mid-5th century | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}} | Excavated items = Cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, and more | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Mishima Aino Mausoleum" (三嶋藍野陵) | Special notes = 21st largest tumulus in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Rankings of Kofun Sizes (National Version)] (Sakai City Website, updated May 13, 2018).
Similar in shape to {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}} (Fujidera, Osaka Prefecture) | Map = Japan Osaka | Label = Oda Chausuyama
Kofun | Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-south-southeast | Label position = top }} '''Oda Chausuyama Kofun''' (太田茶臼山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Oda. It is the largest tumulus in northern Osaka and ranks as the 21st largest in Japan. Though the actual interred individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as "Mishima Aino Mausoleum," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}}. == Overview == The tumulus was constructed on the {{ill|Tonda Plateau|ja|富田台地}} near the right bank of the {{ill|Yodo River|ja|淀川}}. Current management falls under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, which recognizes it as Emperor Keitai's mausoleum. Excavations outside the surrounding moat have been conducted by the Osaka Prefectural and Ibaraki City Boards of Education in 1972 and 1988. The Imperial Household Agency's Sho-Ryo Division also carried out a survey at the mound's base in 2002. The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented south. It measures 226 meters in total length, making it the largest in northern Osaka. Its moat and embankments encircle the mound in a shield shape, with figurative and cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} excavated from its surroundings. Production of these haniwa has been linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Production Site|ja|史跡新池ハニワ工場公園}} in Takatsuki.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第7巻 史料編 考古|2014|pp=293-297}} Other small tombs, believed to be satellite mounds, are located nearby. == Burial Individual == The actual burial individual is unknown, but the Imperial Household Agency attributes the tumulus to Emperor Keitai. Some scholars argue that {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳}} (Takatsuki, Osaka Prefecture) better matches historical records and Emperor Keitai's era.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第1巻 通史|2012|pp=381-389}} == Excavated Items == Artifacts such as cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, and roof tiles have been unearthed. These findings are stored and displayed at the {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館}}. == Cultural Importance == The surrounding moat is designated as part of a national historic site, and the tumulus holds significant cultural value as a testament to the burial practices and social structures of 5th-century Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/026/index.html Mishima Aino Mausoleum] - Imperial Household Agency * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Rankings of Kofun Sizes (Sakai City)] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[ja:太田茶臼山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Oda Chausuyama Kofun | image = [[File:Oda Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (front mound on the left, rear mound in the back right) | Belongs to = {{ill|Mishima Kofun Cluster|ja|三島古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Oda 3-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|50|40.50|N|135|34|42.36|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Dimensions = Total length: 226 meters; height (front mound): 19.8 meters | Construction era = Mid-5th century | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}} | Excavated items = Cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, and more | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Mishima Aino Mausoleum" (三嶋藍野陵) | Special notes = 21st largest tumulus in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Rankings of Kofun Sizes (National Version)] (Sakai City Website, updated May 13, 2018).
Similar in shape to {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}} (Fujidera, Osaka Prefecture) | Map = Japan Osaka | Label = Oda Chausuyama
Kofun | Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-south-southeast | Label position = top }} '''Oda Chausuyama Kofun''' (太田茶臼山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Oda. It is the largest tumulus in northern Osaka and ranks as the 21st largest in Japan. Though the actual interred individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as "Mishima Aino Mausoleum," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}}. == Overview == The tumulus was constructed on the {{ill|Tonda Plateau|ja|富田台地}} near the right bank of the {{ill|Yodo River|ja|淀川}}. Current management falls under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, which recognizes it as Emperor Keitai's mausoleum. Excavations outside the surrounding moat have been conducted by the Osaka Prefectural and Ibaraki City Boards of Education in 1972 and 1988. The Imperial Household Agency's Sho-Ryo Division also carried out a survey at the mound's base in 2002. The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented south. It measures 226 meters in total length, making it the largest in northern Osaka. Its moat and embankments encircle the mound in a shield shape, with figurative and cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} excavated from its surroundings. Production of these haniwa has been linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Production Site|ja|史跡新池ハニワ工場公園}} in Takatsuki.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第7巻 史料編 考古|2014|pp=293-297}} Other small tombs, believed to be satellite mounds, are located nearby. == Burial Individual == The actual burial individual is unknown, but the Imperial Household Agency attributes the tumulus to Emperor Keitai. Some scholars argue that {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳}} (Takatsuki, Osaka Prefecture) better matches historical records and Emperor Keitai's era.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第1巻 通史|2012|pp=381-389}} == Excavated Items == Artifacts such as cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, and roof tiles have been unearthed. These findings are stored and displayed at the {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館}}. == Cultural Importance == The surrounding moat is designated as part of a national historic site, and the tumulus holds significant cultural value as a testament to the burial practices and social structures of 5th-century Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/026/index.html Mishima Aino Mausoleum] - Imperial Household Agency * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Rankings of Kofun Sizes (Sakai City)] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[ja:太田茶臼山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Oda Chausuyama Kofun | image = [[File:Oda Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (front mound on the left, rear mound in the back right) | Belongs to = {{ill|Mishima Kofun Cluster|ja|三島古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Oda 3-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|50|40.50|N|135|34|42.36|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | area = Total length: 226 meters; height (front mound): 19.8 meters | built = Mid-5th century | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}} | excavations = Cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, and more | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Mishima Aino Mausoleum" (三嶋藍野陵) | Special notes = 21st largest tumulus in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Rankings of Kofun areas (National Version)] (Sakai City Website, updated May 13, 2018).
Similar in shape to {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}} (Fujidera, Osaka Prefecture) | map = Japan Osaka | map_dot_label = Oda Chausuyama
Kofun | Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-south-southeast | map_dot_label position = top }} '''Oda Chausuyama Kofun''' (太田茶臼山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Oda. It is the largest tumulus in northern Osaka and ranks as the 21st largest in Japan. Though the actual interred individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as "Mishima Aino Mausoleum," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}}. == Overview == The tumulus was constructed on the {{ill|Tonda Plateau|ja|富田台地}} near the right bank of the {{ill|Yodo River|ja|淀川}}. Current management falls under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, which recognizes it as Emperor Keitai's mausoleum. Excavations outside the surrounding moat have been conducted by the Osaka Prefectural and Ibaraki City Boards of Education in 1972 and 1988. The Imperial Household Agency's Sho-Ryo Division also carried out a survey at the mound's base in 2002. The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented south. It measures 226 meters in total length, making it the largest in northern Osaka. Its moat and embankments encircle the mound in a shield shape, with figurative and cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} excavated from its surroundings. Production of these haniwa has been linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Production Site|ja|史跡新池ハニワ工場公園}} in Takatsuki.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第7巻 史料編 考古|2014|pp=293-297}} Other small tombs, believed to be satellite mounds, are located nearby. == Burial Individual == The actual burial individual is unknown, but the Imperial Household Agency attributes the tumulus to Emperor Keitai. Some scholars argue that {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳}} (Takatsuki, Osaka Prefecture) better matches historical records and Emperor Keitai's era.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第1巻 通史|2012|pp=381-389}} == Excavated Items == Artifacts such as cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, and roof tiles have been unearthed. These findings are stored and displayed at the {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館}}. == Cultural Importance == The surrounding moat is designated as part of a national historic site, and the tumulus holds significant cultural value as a testament to the burial practices and social structures of 5th-century Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/026/index.html Mishima Aino Mausoleum] - Imperial Household Agency * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Rankings of Kofun areas (Sakai City)] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] Kofun (front mound on the left, rear mound in the back right) | Group = {{ill|Mishima Kofun Cluster|ja|三島古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Oda 3-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|50|40.50|N|135|34|42.36|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | area = Total length: 226 meters; height (front mound): 19.8 meters | built = Mid-5th century | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}} | excavations = Cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, and more | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Mishima Aino Mausoleum" (三嶋藍野陵) | Special notes = 21st largest tumulus in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Rankings of Kofun areas (National Version)] (Sakai City Website, updated May 13, 2018).
Similar in shape to {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}} (Fujidera, Osaka Prefecture) | map = Japan Osaka | map_dot_label = Oda Chausuyama
Kofun | Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-south-southeast | map_dot_label position = top }} '''Oda Chausuyama Kofun''' (太田茶臼山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Oda. It is the largest tumulus in northern Osaka and ranks as the 21st largest in Japan. Though the actual interred individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as "Mishima Aino Mausoleum," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}}. == Overview == The tumulus was constructed on the {{ill|Tonda Plateau|ja|富田台地}} near the right bank of the {{ill|Yodo River|ja|淀川}}. Current management falls under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, which recognizes it as Emperor Keitai's mausoleum. Excavations outside the surrounding moat have been conducted by the Osaka Prefectural and Ibaraki City Boards of Education in 1972 and 1988. The Imperial Household Agency's Sho-Ryo Division also carried out a survey at the mound's base in 2002. The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented south. It measures 226 meters in total length, making it the largest in northern Osaka. Its moat and embankments encircle the mound in a shield shape, with figurative and cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} excavated from its surroundings. Production of these haniwa has been linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Production Site|ja|史跡新池ハニワ工場公園}} in Takatsuki.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第7巻 史料編 考古|2014|pp=293-297}} Other small tombs, believed to be satellite mounds, are located nearby. == Burial Individual == The actual burial individual is unknown, but the Imperial Household Agency attributes the tumulus to Emperor Keitai. Some scholars argue that {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳}} (Takatsuki, Osaka Prefecture) better matches historical records and Emperor Keitai's era.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第1巻 通史|2012|pp=381-389}} == Excavated Items == Artifacts such as cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, and roof tiles have been unearthed. These findings are stored and displayed at the {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館}}. == Cultural Importance == The surrounding moat is designated as part of a national historic site, and the tumulus holds significant cultural value as a testament to the burial practices and social structures of 5th-century Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/026/index.html Mishima Aino Mausoleum] - Imperial Household Agency * Production of these haniwa has been linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Production Site|ja|史跡新池ハニワ工場公園}} in Takatsuki.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第7巻 史料編 考古|2014|pp=293-297}} Other small tombs, believed to be satellite mounds, are located nearby. == Burial Individual == The actual burial individual is unknown, but the Imperial Household Agency attributes the tumulus to Emperor Keitai. Some scholars argue that {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳}} (Takatsuki, Osaka Prefecture) better matches historical records and Emperor Keitai's era.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第1巻 通史|2012|pp=381-389}} == Excavated Items == Artifacts such as cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, and roof tiles have been unearthed. These findings are stored and displayed at the {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館}}. == Cultural Importance == The surrounding moat is designated as part of a national historic site, and the tumulus holds significant cultural value as a testament to the burial practices and social structures of 5th-century Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/026/index.html Mishima Aino Mausoleum] - Imperial Household Agency * Kofun | Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-south-southeast | map_dot_label position = top }} '''Oda Chausuyama Kofun''' (太田茶臼山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Oda. It is the largest tumulus in northern Osaka and ranks as the 21st largest in Japan. Though the actual interred individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as "Mishima Aino Mausoleum," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}}. == Overview == The tumulus was constructed on the {{ill|Tonda Plateau|ja|富田台地}} near the right bank of the {{ill|Yodo River|ja|淀川}}. Current management falls under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, which recognizes it as Emperor Keitai's mausoleum. Excavations outside the surrounding moat have been conducted by the Osaka Prefectural and Ibaraki City Boards of Education in 1972 and 1988. The Imperial Household Agency's Sho-Ryo Division also carried out a survey at the mound's base in 2002. The tumulus features a keyhole type, with the front mound oriented south. It measures 226 meters in total length, making it the largest in northern Osaka. Its moat and embankments encircle the mound in a shield type, with figurative and cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} excavated from its surroundings. Production of these haniwa has been linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Production Site|ja|史跡新池ハニワ工場公園}} in Takatsuki.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第7巻 史料編 考古|2014|pp=293-297}} Other small tombs, believed to be satellite mounds, are located nearby. == Burial Individual == The actual burial individual is unknown, but the Imperial Household Agency attributes the tumulus to Emperor Keitai. Some scholars argue that {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳}} (Takatsuki, Osaka Prefecture) better matches historical records and Emperor Keitai's era.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第1巻 通史|2012|pp=381-389}} == Excavated Items == Artifacts such as cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, and roof tiles have been unearthed. These findings are stored and displayed at the {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館}}. == Cultural Importance == The surrounding moat is designated as part of a national historic site, and the tumulus holds significant cultural value as a testament to the burial practices and social structures of 5th-century Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/026/index.html Mishima Aino Mausoleum] - Imperial Household Agency * Kofun | Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-south-southeast | map_dot_label position = top }} '''Oda Chausuyama Kofun''' (太田茶臼山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Oda. It is the largest tumulus in northern Osaka and ranks as the 21st largest in Japan. Though the actual interred individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as "Mishima Aino Mausoleum," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}}. == Overview == The tumulus was constructed on the {{ill|Tonda Plateau|ja|富田台地}} near the right bank of the {{ill|Yodo River|ja|淀川}}. Current management falls under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, which recognizes it as Emperor Keitai's mausoleum. Excavations outside the surrounding moat have been conducted by the Osaka Prefectural and Ibaraki City Boards of Education in 1972 and 1988. The Imperial Household Agency's Sho-Ryo Division also carried out a survey at the mound's base in 2002. The tumulus features a keyhole type, with the front mound oriented south. It measures 226 meters in total length, making it the largest in northern Osaka. Its moat and embankments encircle the mound in a shield type, with figurative and cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} excavated from its surroundings. Production of these haniwa has been linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Production Site|ja|史跡新池ハニワ工場公園}} in Takatsuki.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第7巻 史料編 考古|2014|pp=293-297}} Other small tombs, believed to be satellite mounds, are located nearby. == Burial Individual == The actual burial individual is unknown, but the Imperial Household Agency attributes the tumulus to Emperor Keitai. Some scholars argue that {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳}} (Takatsuki, Osaka Prefecture) better matches historical records and Emperor Keitai's era.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第1巻 通史|2012|pp=381-389}} == Excavated Items == Artifacts such as cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, and roof tiles have been unearthed. These findings are stored and displayed at the {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館}}. == Cultural Importance == The surrounding moat is designated as part of a national historic site, and the tumulus holds significant cultural value as a testament to the burial practices and social structures of 5th-century Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/026/index.html Mishima Aino Mausoleum] - Imperial Household Agency * Kofun (front mound on the left, rear mound in the back right) | part_of = {{ill|Mishima Kofun Cluster|ja|三島古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Oda 3-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|50|40.50|N|135|34|42.36|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | area = Total length: 226 meters; height (front mound): 19.8 meters | built = Mid-5th century | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}} | excavations = Cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, and more | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Mishima Aino Mausoleum" (三嶋藍野陵) | notes = 21st largest tumulus in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Rankings of Kofun areas (National Version)] (Sakai City Website, updated May 13, 2018).
Similar in type to {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}} (Fujidera, Osaka Prefecture) | map = Japan Osaka | map_dot_label = Oda Chausuyama
Kofun | Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-south-southeast | map_dot_label position = top }} '''Oda Chausuyama Kofun''' (太田茶臼山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Oda. It is the largest tumulus in northern Osaka and ranks as the 21st largest in Japan. Though the actual interred individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as "Mishima Aino Mausoleum," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}}. == Overview == The tumulus was constructed on the {{ill|Tonda Plateau|ja|富田台地}} near the right bank of the {{ill|Yodo River|ja|淀川}}. Current management falls under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, which recognizes it as Emperor Keitai's mausoleum. Excavations outside the surrounding moat have been conducted by the Osaka Prefectural and Ibaraki City Boards of Education in 1972 and 1988. The Imperial Household Agency's Sho-Ryo Division also carried out a survey at the mound's base in 2002. The tumulus features a keyhole type, with the front mound oriented south. It measures 226 meters in total length, making it the largest in northern Osaka. Its moat and embankments encircle the mound in a shield type, with figurative and cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} excavated from its surroundings. Production of these haniwa has been linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Production Site|ja|史跡新池ハニワ工場公園}} in Takatsuki.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第7巻 史料編 考古|2014|pp=293-297}} Other small tombs, believed to be satellite mounds, are located nearby. == Burial Individual == The actual burial individual is unknown, but the Imperial Household Agency attributes the tumulus to Emperor Keitai. Some scholars argue that {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳}} (Takatsuki, Osaka Prefecture) better matches historical records and Emperor Keitai's era.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第1巻 通史|2012|pp=381-389}} == Excavated Items == Artifacts such as cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, and roof tiles have been unearthed. These findings are stored and displayed at the {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館}}. == Cultural Importance == The surrounding moat is designated as part of a national historic site, and the tumulus holds significant cultural value as a testament to the burial practices and social structures of 5th-century Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/026/index.html Mishima Aino Mausoleum] - Imperial Household Agency * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Rankings of Kofun areas (Sakai City)] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] Kofun (front mound on the left, rear mound in the back right) | part_of = {{ill|Mishima Kofun Cluster|ja|三島古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Oda 3-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|50|40.50|N|135|34|42.36|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | area = Total length: 226 meters; height (front mound): 19.8 meters | built = Mid-5th century | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}} | excavations = Cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, and more | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Mishima Aino Mausoleum" (三嶋藍野陵) | notes = 21st largest tumulus in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Rankings of Kofun areas (National Version)] (Sakai City Website, updated May 13, 2018).
Similar in type to {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}} (Fujidera, Osaka Prefecture) | map = Japan Osaka | map_dot_label = Oda Chausuyama
Kofun | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-south-southeast | map_dot_label position = top }} '''Oda Chausuyama Kofun''' (太田茶臼山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Oda. It is the largest tumulus in northern Osaka and ranks as the 21st largest in Japan. Though the actual interred individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as "Mishima Aino Mausoleum," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}}. == Overview == The tumulus was constructed on the {{ill|Tonda Plateau|ja|富田台地}} near the right bank of the {{ill|Yodo River|ja|淀川}}. Current management falls under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, which recognizes it as Emperor Keitai's mausoleum. Excavations outside the surrounding moat have been conducted by the Osaka Prefectural and Ibaraki City Boards of Education in 1972 and 1988. The Imperial Household Agency's Sho-Ryo Division also carried out a survey at the mound's base in 2002. The tumulus features a keyhole type, with the front mound oriented south. It measures 226 meters in total length, making it the largest in northern Osaka. Its moat and embankments encircle the mound in a shield type, with figurative and cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} excavated from its surroundings. Production of these haniwa has been linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Production Site|ja|史跡新池ハニワ工場公園}} in Takatsuki.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第7巻 史料編 考古|2014|pp=293-297}} Other small tombs, believed to be satellite mounds, are located nearby. == Burial Individual == The actual burial individual is unknown, but the Imperial Household Agency attributes the tumulus to Emperor Keitai. Some scholars argue that {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳}} (Takatsuki, Osaka Prefecture) better matches historical records and Emperor Keitai's era.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第1巻 通史|2012|pp=381-389}} == Excavated Items == Artifacts such as cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, and roof tiles have been unearthed. These findings are stored and displayed at the {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館}}. == Cultural Importance == The surrounding moat is designated as part of a national historic site, and the tumulus holds significant cultural value as a testament to the burial practices and social structures of 5th-century Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/026/index.html Mishima Aino Mausoleum] - Imperial Household Agency * revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[ja:太田茶臼山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Oda Chausuyama Kofun | image = [[File:Oda Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (front mound on the left, rear mound in the back right) | part_of = {{ill|Mishima Kofun Cluster|ja|三島古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Oda 3-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|50|40.50|N|135|34|42.36|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | area = Total length: 226 meters; height (front mound): 19.8 meters | built = Mid-5th century | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}} | excavations = Cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, and more | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Mishima Aino Mausoleum" (三嶋藍野陵) | notes = 21st largest tumulus in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Rankings of Kofun areas (National Version)] (Sakai City Website, updated May 13, 2018).
Similar in type to {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}} (Fujidera, Osaka Prefecture) | map = Japan Osaka | map_dot_label = Oda Chausuyama
Kofun | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-south-southeast | map_dot_label position = top }} '''Oda Chausuyama Kofun''' (太田茶臼山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Oda. It is the largest tumulus in northern Osaka and ranks as the 21st largest in Japan. Though the actual interred individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as "Mishima Aino Mausoleum," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}}. == Overview == The tumulus was constructed on the {{ill|Tonda Plateau|ja|富田台地}} near the right bank of the {{ill|Yodo River|ja|淀川}}. Current management falls under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, which recognizes it as Emperor Keitai's mausoleum. Excavations outside the surrounding moat have been conducted by the Osaka Prefectural and Ibaraki City Boards of Education in 1972 and 1988. The Imperial Household Agency's Sho-Ryo Division also carried out a survey at the mound's base in 2002. The tumulus features a keyhole type, with the front mound oriented south. It measures 226 meters in total length, making it the largest in northern Osaka. Its moat and embankments encircle the mound in a shield type, with figurative and cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} excavated from its surroundings. Production of these haniwa has been linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Production Site|ja|史跡新池ハニワ工場公園}} in Takatsuki.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第7巻 史料編 考古|2014|pp=293-297}} Other small tombs, believed to be satellite mounds, are located nearby. == Burial Individual == The actual burial individual is unknown, but the Imperial Household Agency attributes the tumulus to Emperor Keitai. Some scholars argue that {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳}} (Takatsuki, Osaka Prefecture) better matches historical records and Emperor Keitai's era.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第1巻 通史|2012|pp=381-389}} == Excavated Items == burial goods such as cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, and roof tiles have been unearthed. These findings are stored and displayed at the {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館}}. == Cultural Importance == The surrounding moat is designation1 as part of a national historic site, and the tumulus holds significant cultural value as a testament to the burial practices and social structures of 5th-century Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/026/index.html Mishima Aino Mausoleum] - Imperial Household Agency * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Rankings of Kofun areas (Sakai City)] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] It is the largest tumulus in northern Osaka and ranks as the 21st largest in Japan. Though the actual interred individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as "Mishima Aino Mausoleum," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}}. == Overview == The tumulus was constructed on the {{ill|Tonda Plateau|ja|富田台地}} near the right bank of the {{ill|Yodo River|ja|淀川}}. Current management falls under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, which recognizes it as Emperor Keitai's mausoleum. Excavations outside the surrounding moat have been conducted by the Osaka Prefectural and Ibaraki City Boards of Education in 1972 and 1988. The Imperial Household Agency's Sho-Ryo Division also carried out a survey at the mound's base in 2002. The tumulus features a keyhole type, with the front mound oriented south. It measures 226 meters in total length, making it the largest in northern Osaka. Its moat and embankments encircle the mound in a shield type, with figurative and cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} excavated from its surroundings. Production of these haniwa has been linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Production Site|ja|史跡新池ハニワ工場公園}} in Takatsuki.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第7巻 史料編 考古|2014|pp=293-297}} Other small tombs, believed to be satellite mounds, are located nearby. == Burial Individual == The actual burial individual is unknown, but the Imperial Household Agency attributes the tumulus to Emperor Keitai. Some scholars argue that {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳}} (Takatsuki, Osaka Prefecture) better matches historical records and Emperor Keitai's era.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第1巻 通史|2012|pp=381-389}} == Excavated Items == burial goods such as cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, and roof tiles have been unearthed. These findings are stored and displayed at the {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館}}. == Cultural Importance == The surrounding moat is other_designation1 as part of a national historic site, and the tumulus holds significant cultural value as a testament to the burial practices and social structures of 5th-century Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Facilities == * Kofun (front mound on the left, rear mound in the back right) | part_of = {{ill|Mishima Kofun Cluster|ja|三島古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Oda 3-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|50|40.50|N|135|34|42.36|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | area = Total length: 226 meters; height (front mound): 19.8 meters | built = Mid-5th century | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} other_designation) {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}} | excavations = Cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, and more | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Mishima Aino Mausoleum" (三嶋藍野陵) | notes = 21st largest tumulus in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Rankings of Kofun areas (National Version)] (Sakai City Website, updated May 13, 2018).
Similar in type to {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}} (Fujidera, Osaka Prefecture) | map = Japan Osaka | map_dot_label = Oda Chausuyama
Kofun | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-south-southeast | map_dot_label = top }} '''Oda Chausuyama Kofun''' (太田茶臼山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Oda. It is the largest tumulus in northern Osaka and ranks as the 21st largest in Japan. Though the actual interred individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as "Mishima Aino Mausoleum," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}}. == Overview == The tumulus was constructed on the {{ill|Tonda Plateau|ja|富田台地}} near the right bank of the {{ill|Yodo River|ja|淀川}}. Current management falls under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, which recognizes it as Emperor Keitai's mausoleum. Excavations outside the surrounding moat have been conducted by the Osaka Prefectural and Ibaraki City Boards of Education in 1972 and 1988. The Imperial Household Agency's Sho-Ryo Division also carried out a survey at the mound's base in 2002. The tumulus features a keyhole type, with the front mound oriented south. It measures 226 meters in total length, making it the largest in northern Osaka. Its moat and embankments encircle the mound in a shield type, with figurative and cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} excavated from its surroundings. Production of these haniwa has been linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Production Site|ja|史跡新池ハニワ工場公園}} in Takatsuki.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第7巻 史料編 考古|2014|pp=293-297}} Other small tombs, believed to be satellite mounds, are located nearby. == Burial Individual == The actual burial individual is unknown, but the Imperial Household Agency attributes the tumulus to Emperor Keitai. Some scholars argue that {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳}} (Takatsuki, Osaka Prefecture) better matches historical records and Emperor Keitai's era.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第1巻 通史|2012|pp=381-389}} == Excavated Items == burial goods such as cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, and roof tiles have been unearthed. These findings are stored and displayed at the {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館}}. == Cultural Importance == The surrounding moat is other_designation1 as part of a national historic site, and the tumulus holds significant cultural value as a testament to the burial practices and social structures of 5th-century Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Facilities == *
