So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Oda Chausuyama Kofun | image = [[File:Oda Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (front mound on the left, rear mound in the back right) | part_of = {{ill|Mishima Kofun Cluster|ja|三島古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima Kofun Cluster|qq=}} | location = {{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki, Osaka|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture, Oda 3-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|50|40.50|N|135|34|42.36|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} | area = Total length: 226 meters; height (front mound): 19.8 meters | built = Mid-5th century | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} Historic Site) {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Keitai|qq=}} | excavations = Cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, and more | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Mishima Aino Mausoleum" (三嶋藍野陵) | notes = 21st largest tumulus in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Rankings of Kofun areas (National Version)] (Sakai City Website, updated May 13, 2018).
Similar in type to {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinoyama Kofun|qq=}} (Fujidera, Osaka Prefecture) | map = Japan Osaka | map_dot_label = Oda Chausuyama
Kofun | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-south-southeast | map_dot_label = top }} '''Oda Chausuyama Kofun''' (太田茶臼山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki, Osaka|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture, Oda. It is the largest tumulus in northern Osaka and Ranks as the 21st largest in Japan. Though the actual interred individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} designates it as "Mishima Aino Mausoleum," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Keitai|qq=}}. == Overview == The tumulus was constructed on the {{ill|Tonda Plateau|ja|富田台地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tonda Plateau|qq=}} near the right bank of the {{ill|Yodo River|ja|淀川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yodo River|qq=}}. Current management falls under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, which recognizes it as Emperor Keitai's mausoleum. Excavations outside the surrounding moat have been conducted by the Osaka Prefectural and Ibaraki City Boards of Education in 1972 and 1988. The Imperial Household Agency's Sho-Ryo Division also carried out a survey at the mound's base in 2002. The tumulus features a keyhole type, with the front mound oriented south. It measures 226 meters in total length, making it the largest in northern Osaka. Its moat and embankments encircle the mound in a shield type, with figurative and cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}} excavated from its surroundings. Production of these haniwa has been linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Production Site|ja|史跡新池ハニワ工場公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinike Haniwa Production Site|qq=}} in Takatsuki.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第7巻 史料編 考古|2014|pp=293-297}} Other small tombs, believed to be satellite mounds, are located nearby. == Burial Individual == The actual burial individual is unknown, but the Imperial Household Agency attributes the tumulus to Emperor Keitai. Some scholars argue that {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imashirozuka Kofun|qq=}} (Takatsuki, Osaka Prefecture) better matches historical records and Emperor Keitai's era.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第1巻 通史|2012|pp=381-389}} == Excavated Items == burial goods such as cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, and roof tiles have been unearthed. These findings are stored and displayed at the {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Municipal Museum|qq=}}. == Cultural Importance == The surrounding moat is Historic Site1 as part of a national historic site, and the tumulus holds significant cultural value as a testament to the burial practices and social structures of 5th-century Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Municipal Museum|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/026/index.html Mishima Aino Mausoleum] - Imperial Household Agency * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Rankings of Kofun areas (Sakai City)] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|太田茶臼山古墳|version=103903640|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Ibaraki, Osaka]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture's historical sites]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Ota Chausuyama Kofun]] [[Category:Oda Chausuyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Ōta Chausuyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Ōta Chausuyama Kofun]] [[ja:太田茶臼山古墳]] [[en:Ōta Chausuyama Kofun]]

These findings are stored and displayed at the {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Municipal Museum|qq=}}. == Cultural Importance == The surrounding moat is Historic Site1 as part of a national historic site, and the tumulus holds significant cultural value as a testament to the burial practices and social structures of 5th-century Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Municipal Museum|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/026/index.html Mishima Aino Mausoleum] - Imperial Household Agency * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Rankings of Kofun areas (Sakai City)] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|太田茶臼山古墳|version=103903640|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Ibaraki, Osaka]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture's historical sites]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Ota Chausuyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Ōta Chausuyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Ōta Chausuyama Kofun]] [[ja:太田茶臼山古墳]] [[en:Ōta Chausuyama Kofun]]

Kofun | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-south-southeast | map_dot_label = top }} '''Oda Chausuyama Kofun''' (太田茶臼山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki, Osaka|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture, Oda. It is the largest tumulus in northern Osaka and Ranks as the 21st largest in Japan. Though the actual interred individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} designates it as "Mishima Aino Mausoleum," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Keitai|qq=}}. == Overview == The tumulus was constructed on the {{ill|Tonda Plateau|ja|富田台地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tonda Plateau|qq=}} near the right bank of the {{ill|Yodo River|ja|淀川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yodo River|qq=}}. Current management falls under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, which recognizes it as Emperor Keitai's mausoleum. Excavations outside the surrounding moat have been conducted by the Osaka Prefectural and Ibaraki City Boards of Education in 1972 and 1988. The Imperial Household Agency's Sho-Ryo Division also carried out a survey at the mound's base in 2002. The tumulus features a keyhole type, with the front mound oriented south. It measures 226 meters in total length, making it the largest in northern Osaka. Its moat and embankments encircle the mound in a shield type, with figurative and cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}} excavated from its surroundings. Production of these haniwa has been linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Production Site|ja|史跡新池ハニワ工場公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinike Haniwa Production Site|qq=}} in Takatsuki.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第7巻 史料編 考古|2014|pp=293-297}} Other small tombs, believed to be satellite mounds, are located nearby. == Burial Individual == The actual burial individual is unknown, but the Imperial Household Agency attributes the tumulus to Emperor Keitai. Some scholars argue that {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imashirozuka Kofun|qq=}} (Takatsuki, Osaka Prefecture) better matches historical records and Emperor Keitai's era.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第1巻 通史|2012|pp=381-389}} == Excavated Items == burial goods such as cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, and roof tiles have been unearthed. These findings are stored and displayed at the {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Municipal Museum|qq=}}. == Cultural Importance == The surrounding moat is Historic Site1 as part of a national historic site, and the tumulus holds significant cultural value as a testament to the burial practices and social structures of 5th-century Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Municipal Museum|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/026/index.html Mishima Aino Mausoleum] - Imperial Household Agency * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Rankings of Kofun areas (Sakai City)] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|太田茶臼山古墳|version=103903640|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Ibaraki, Osaka]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture's historical sites]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Ōta Chausuyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Ōta Chausuyama Kofun]] [[ja:太田茶臼山古墳]] [[en:Ōta Chausuyama Kofun]]

It is the largest tumulus in northern Osaka and Ranks as the 21st largest in Japan. Though the actual interred individual is unknown, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as "Mishima Aino Mausoleum," attributed to [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]]. == Overview == The tumulus was constructed on the {{ill|Tonda Plateau|ja|富田台地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tonda Plateau|qq=}} near the right bank of the [[:en:Yodo River|Yodo River]]. Kofun (front mound on the left, rear mound in the back right) | part_of = {{ill|Mishima Kofun Cluster|ja|三島古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima Kofun Cluster|qq=}} | location = [[:en:Ibaraki, Osaka|Ibaraki, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture, Oda 3-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|50|40.50|N|135|34|42.36|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | area = Total length: 226 meters; height (front mound): 19.8 meters | built = Mid-5th century | Interred_individual = ([[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site) [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]] | excavations = Cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, and more | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Mishima Aino Mausoleum" (三嶋藍野陵) | notes = 21st largest tumulus in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Rankings of Kofun areas (National Version)] (Sakai City Website, updated May 13, 2018).
Similar in type to [[:en:Ichinoyama Kofun|Ichinoyama Kofun]] (Fujidera, Osaka Prefecture) | map = Japan Osaka | map_dot_label = Oda Chausuyama
Kofun | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-south-southeast | map_dot_label = top }} '''Oda Chausuyama Kofun''' (太田茶臼山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Ibaraki, Osaka|Ibaraki, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture, Oda. It is the largest tumulus in northern Osaka and Ranks as the 21st largest in Japan. Though the actual interred individual is unknown, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as "Mishima Aino Mausoleum," attributed to [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]]. == Overview == The tumulus was constructed on the {{ill|Tonda Plateau|ja|富田台地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tonda Plateau|qq=}} near the right bank of the [[:en:Yodo River|Yodo River]]. Current management falls under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, which recognizes it as Emperor Keitai's mausoleum. Excavations outside the surrounding moat have been conducted by the Osaka Prefectural and Ibaraki City Boards of Education in 1972 and 1988. The Imperial Household Agency's Sho-Ryo Division also carried out a survey at the mound's base in 2002. The tumulus features a keyhole type, with the front mound oriented south. It measures 226 meters in total length, making it the largest in northern Osaka. Its moat and embankments encircle the mound in a shield type, with figurative and cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] excavated from its surroundings. Production of these haniwa has been linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Production Site|ja|史跡新池ハニワ工場公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinike Haniwa Production Site|qq=}} in Takatsuki.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第7巻 史料編 考古|2014|pp=293-297}} Other small tombs, believed to be satellite mounds, are located nearby. == Burial Individual == The actual burial individual is unknown, but the Imperial Household Agency attributes the tumulus to Emperor Keitai. Some scholars argue that [[:en:Imashirozuka Kofun|Imashirozuka Kofun]] (Takatsuki, Osaka Prefecture) better matches historical records and Emperor Keitai's era.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第1巻 通史|2012|pp=381-389}} == Excavated Items == burial goods such as cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, and roof tiles have been unearthed. These findings are stored and displayed at the {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Municipal Museum|qq=}}. == Cultural Importance == The surrounding moat is Historic Site1 as part of a national historic site, and the tumulus holds significant cultural value as a testament to the burial practices and social structures of 5th-century Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Municipal Museum|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/026/index.html Mishima Aino Mausoleum] - Imperial Household Agency * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Rankings of Kofun areas (Sakai City)] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|太田茶臼山古墳|version=103903640|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Ibaraki, Osaka]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture's historical sites]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Ōta Chausuyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Ōta Chausuyama Kofun]] [[ja:太田茶臼山古墳]] [[en:Ōta Chausuyama Kofun]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]]

Kofun (front mound on the left, rear mound in the back right) | part_of = {{ill|Mishima Kofun Cluster|ja|三島古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima Kofun Cluster|qq=}} | location = [[:en:Ibaraki, Osaka|Ibaraki, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture, Oda 3-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|50|40.50|N|135|34|42.36|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | area = Total length: 226 meters; height (front mound): 19.8 meters | built = Mid-5th century | Interred_individual = ([[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site) [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]] | excavations = Cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, and more | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Mishima Aino Mausoleum" (三嶋藍野陵) | notes = 21st largest tumulus in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Rankings of Kofun areas (National Version)] (Sakai City Website, updated May 13, 2018).
Similar in type to [[:en:Ichinoyama Kofun|Ichinoyama Kofun]] (Fujidera, Osaka Prefecture) | map = Japan Osaka | map_dot_label = Oda Chausuyama
Kofun | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-south-southeast | map_dot_label = top }} '''Oda Chausuyama Kofun''' (太田茶臼山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Ibaraki, Osaka|Ibaraki, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture, Oda. It is the largest tumulus in northern Osaka and Ranks as the 21st largest in Japan. Though the actual interred individual is unknown, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as "Mishima Aino Mausoleum," attributed to [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]]. == Overview == The tumulus was constructed on the {{ill|Tonda Plateau|ja|富田台地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tonda Plateau|qq=}} near the right bank of the [[:en:Yodo River|Yodo River]]. Current management falls under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, which recognizes it as Emperor Keitai's mausoleum. Excavations outside the surrounding moat have been conducted by the Osaka Prefectural and Ibaraki City Boards of Education in 1972 and 1988. The Imperial Household Agency's Sho-Ryo Division also carried out a survey at the mound's base in 2002. The tumulus features a keyhole type, with the front mound oriented south. It measures 226 meters in total length, making it the largest in northern Osaka. Its moat and embankments encircle the mound in a shield type, with figurative and cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] excavated from its surroundings. Production of these haniwa has been linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Production Site|ja|史跡新池ハニワ工場公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinike Haniwa Production Site|qq=}} in Takatsuki.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第7巻 史料編 考古|2014|pp=293-297}} Other small tombs, believed to be satellite mounds, are located nearby. == Burial Individual == The actual burial individual is unknown, but the Imperial Household Agency attributes the tumulus to Emperor Keitai. Some scholars argue that [[:en:Imashirozuka Kofun|Imashirozuka Kofun]] (Takatsuki, Osaka Prefecture) better matches historical records and Emperor Keitai's era.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第1巻 通史|2012|pp=381-389}} == Excavated Items == burial goods such as cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, and roof tiles have been unearthed. These findings are stored and displayed at the {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Municipal Museum|qq=}}. == Cultural Importance == The surrounding moat is Historic Site1 as part of a national historic site, and the tumulus holds significant cultural value as a testament to the burial practices and social structures of 5th-century Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Municipal Museum|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/026/index.html Mishima Aino Mausoleum] - Imperial Household Agency * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Rankings of Kofun areas (Sakai City)] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|太田茶臼山古墳|version=103903640|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Ibaraki, Osaka]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture's historical sites]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Ōta Chausuyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Ōta Chausuyama Kofun]] [[ja:太田茶臼山古墳]] [[en:Ōta Chausuyama Kofun]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Emperor Keitai]] [[Category:History of Ibaraki, Osaka]]

Excavations outside the surrounding moat have been conducted by the Osaka Prefectural and Ibaraki City Boards of Education in 1972 and 1988. The Imperial Household Agency's Sho-Ryo Division also carried out a survey at the mound's base in 2002. The tumulus features a keyhole type, with the front mound oriented south. It measures 226 meters in total length, making it the largest in northern Osaka. Its moat and embankments encircle the mound in a shield type, with figurative and cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] excavated from its surroundings. Production of these haniwa has been linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Production Site|ja|史跡新池ハニワ工場公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinike Haniwa Production Site|qq=}} in Takatsuki.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第7巻 史料編 考古|2014|pp=293-297}} Other small tombs, believed to be satellite mounds, are located nearby. == Burial Individual == The actual burial individual is unknown, but the Imperial Household Agency attributes the tumulus to Emperor Keitai. Some scholars argue that [[:en:Imashirozuka Kofun|Imashirozuka Kofun]] (Takatsuki, Osaka Prefecture) better matches historical records and Emperor Keitai's era.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第1巻 通史|2012|pp=381-389}} == Excavated Items == burial goods such as cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, and roof tiles have been unearthed. These findings are stored and displayed at the {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Municipal Museum|qq=}}. == Cultural Importance == The surrounding moat is Historic Site1 as part of a national historic site, and the tumulus holds significant cultural value as a testament to the burial practices and social structures of 5th-century Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Municipal Museum|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/026/index.html Mishima Aino Mausoleum] - Imperial Household Agency * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Rankings of Kofun areas (Sakai City)] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|太田茶臼山古墳|version=103903640|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Ibaraki, Osaka]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture's historical sites]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Ōta Chausuyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Ōta Chausuyama Kofun]] [[ja:太田茶臼山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture�]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Emperor Keitai]] [[Category:History of Ibaraki, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Oda Chausuyama Kofun}}

Kofun (front mound on the left, rear mound in the back right) | part_of = {{ill|Mishima Kofun Cluster|ja|三島古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima Kofun Cluster|qq=}} | location = [[:en:Ibaraki, Osaka|Ibaraki, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture, Oda 3-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|50|40.50|N|135|34|42.36|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | area = Total length: 226 meters; height (front mound): 19.8 meters | built = Mid-5th century | Interred_individual = ([[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site) [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]] | excavations = Cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, and more | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Mishima Aino Mausoleum" (三嶋藍野陵) | notes = 21st largest tumulus in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Rankings of Kofun areas (National Version)] (Sakai City Website, updated May 13, 2018).
Similar in type to [[:en:Ichinoyama Kofun|Ichinoyama Kofun]] (Fujidera, Osaka Prefecture) | map = Japan Osaka | map_dot_label = Oda Chausuyama
Kofun | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-south-southeast | map_dot_label = top }} '''Oda Chausuyama Kofun''' (太田茶臼山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Ibaraki, Osaka|Ibaraki, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture, Oda. It is the largest tumulus in northern Osaka and Ranks as the 21st largest in Japan. Though the actual interred individual is unknown, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as "Mishima Aino Mausoleum," attributed to [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]]. == Overview == The tumulus was constructed on the {{ill|Tonda Plateau|ja|富田台地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tonda Plateau|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} near the right bank of the [[:en:Yodo River|Yodo River]]. Current management falls under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, which recognizes it as Emperor Keitai's mausoleum. Excavations outside the surrounding moat have been conducted by the Osaka Prefectural and Ibaraki City Boards of Education in 1972 and 1988. The Imperial Household Agency's Sho-Ryo Division also carried out a survey at the mound's base in 2002. The tumulus features a keyhole type, with the front mound oriented south. It measures 226 meters in total length, making it the largest in northern Osaka. Its moat and embankments encircle the mound in a shield type, with figurative and cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] excavated from its surroundings. Production of these haniwa has been linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Production Site|ja|史跡新池ハニワ工場公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinike Haniwa Production Site|qq=}} in Takatsuki.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第7巻 史料編 考古|2014|pp=293-297}} Other small tombs, believed to be satellite mounds, are located nearby. == Burial Individual == The actual burial individual is unknown, but the Imperial Household Agency attributes the tumulus to Emperor Keitai. Some scholars argue that [[:en:Imashirozuka Kofun|Imashirozuka Kofun]] (Takatsuki, Osaka Prefecture) better matches historical records and Emperor Keitai's era.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第1巻 通史|2012|pp=381-389}} == Excavated Items == burial goods such as cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, and roof tiles have been unearthed. These findings are stored and displayed at the {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Municipal Museum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Cultural Importance == The surrounding moat is Historic Site1 as part of a national historic site, and the tumulus holds significant cultural value as a testament to the burial practices and social structures of 5th-century Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Municipal Museum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/026/index.html Mishima Aino Mausoleum] - Imperial Household Agency * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Rankings of Kofun areas (Sakai City)] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|太田茶臼山古墳|version=103903640|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Ibaraki, Osaka]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture's historical sites]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Ōta Chausuyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Ōta Chausuyama Kofun]] [[ja:太田茶臼山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Emperor Keitai]] [[Category:History of Ibaraki, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Oda Chausuyama Kofun}}

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11444409]] Wikidata name: Ōda Chausuyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Oda Chausuyama Kofun | image = [[File:Oda Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (front mound on the left, rear mound in the back right) | part_of = {{ill|Mishima Kofun Cluster|ja|三島古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima Kofun Cluster|qq=}} | location = [[:en:Ibaraki, Osaka|Ibaraki, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture, Oda 3-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|50|40.50|N|135|34|42.36|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | area = Total length: 226 meters; height (front mound): 19.8 meters | built = Mid-5th century | Interred_individual = ([[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site) [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]] | excavations = Cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, and more | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Mishima Aino Mausoleum" (三嶋藍野陵) | notes = 21st largest tumulus in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Rankings of Kofun areas (National Version)] (Sakai City Website, updated May 13, 2018).
Similar in type to [[:en:Ichinoyama Kofun|Ichinoyama Kofun]] (Fujidera, Osaka Prefecture) | map = Japan Osaka | map_dot_label = Oda Chausuyama
Kofun | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-south-southeast | map_dot_label = top }} '''Oda Chausuyama Kofun''' (太田茶臼山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Ibaraki, Osaka|Ibaraki, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture, Oda. It is the largest tumulus in northern Osaka and Ranks as the 21st largest in Japan. Though the actual interred individual is unknown, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as "Mishima Aino Mausoleum," attributed to [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]]. == Overview == The tumulus was constructed on the {{ill|Tonda Plateau|ja|富田台地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tonda Plateau|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} near the right bank of the [[:en:Yodo River|Yodo River]]. Current management falls under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, which recognizes it as Emperor Keitai's mausoleum. Excavations outside the surrounding moat have been conducted by the Osaka Prefectural and Ibaraki City Boards of Education in 1972 and 1988. The Imperial Household Agency's Sho-Ryo Division also carried out a survey at the mound's base in 2002. The tumulus features a keyhole type, with the front mound oriented south. It measures 226 meters in total length, making it the largest in northern Osaka. Its moat and embankments encircle the mound in a shield type, with figurative and cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] excavated from its surroundings. Production of these haniwa has been linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Production Site|ja|史跡新池ハニワ工場公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinike Haniwa Production Site|qq=}} in Takatsuki.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第7巻 史料編 考古|2014|pp=293-297}} Other small tombs, believed to be satellite mounds, are located nearby. == Burial Individual == The actual burial individual is unknown, but the Imperial Household Agency attributes the tumulus to Emperor Keitai. Some scholars argue that [[:en:Imashirozuka Kofun|Imashirozuka Kofun]] (Takatsuki, Osaka Prefecture) better matches historical records and Emperor Keitai's era.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第1巻 通史|2012|pp=381-389}} == Excavated Items == burial goods such as cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, and roof tiles have been unearthed. These findings are stored and displayed at the {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Municipal Museum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Cultural Importance == The surrounding moat is Historic Site1 as part of a national historic site, and the tumulus holds significant cultural value as a testament to the burial practices and social structures of 5th-century Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Municipal Museum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/026/index.html Mishima Aino Mausoleum] - Imperial Household Agency * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Rankings of Kofun areas (Sakai City)] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|太田茶臼山古墳|version=103903640|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Ibaraki, Osaka]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[sn:Ōta Chausuyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Ōta Chausuyama Kofun]] [[ja:太田茶臼山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Emperor Keitai]] [[Category:History of Ibaraki, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Oda Chausuyama Kofun}}

Similar in type to [[:en:Ichinoyama Kofun|Ichinoyama Kofun]] (Fujidera, Osaka Prefecture) | map = Japan Osaka | map_dot_label = Oda Chausuyama
Kofun | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-south-southeast | map_dot_label = top | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | commemorates = {{ill|Keitai|qid=Q349277}} }} '''Oda Chausuyama Kofun''' (太田茶臼山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Ibaraki, Osaka|Ibaraki, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture, Oda. It is the largest tumulus in northern Osaka and Ranks as the 21st largest in Japan. Though the actual interred individual is unknown, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as "Mishima Aino Mausoleum," attributed to [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]]. == Overview == The tumulus was constructed on the {{ill|Tonda Plateau|ja|富田台地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tonda Plateau|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} near the right bank of the [[:en:Yodo River|Yodo River]]. Current management falls under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, which recognizes it as Emperor Keitai's mausoleum. Excavations outside the surrounding moat have been conducted by the Osaka Prefectural and Ibaraki City Boards of Education in 1972 and 1988. The Imperial Household Agency's Sho-Ryo Division also carried out a survey at the mound's base in 2002. The tumulus features a keyhole type, with the front mound oriented south. It measures 226 meters in total length, making it the largest in northern Osaka. Its moat and embankments encircle the mound in a shield type, with figurative and cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] excavated from its surroundings. Production of these haniwa has been linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Production Site|ja|史跡新池ハニワ工場公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinike Haniwa Production Site|qq=}} in Takatsuki.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第7巻 史料編 考古|2014|pp=293-297}} Other small tombs, believed to be satellite mounds, are located nearby. == Burial Individual == The actual burial individual is unknown, but the Imperial Household Agency attributes the tumulus to Emperor Keitai. Some scholars argue that [[:en:Imashirozuka Kofun|Imashirozuka Kofun]] (Takatsuki, Osaka Prefecture) better matches historical records and Emperor Keitai's era.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第1巻 通史|2012|pp=381-389}} == Excavated Items == burial goods such as cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, and roof tiles have been unearthed. These findings are stored and displayed at the {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Municipal Museum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Cultural Importance == The surrounding moat is Historic Site1 as part of a national historic site, and the tumulus holds significant cultural value as a testament to the burial practices and social structures of 5th-century Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Municipal Museum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/026/index.html Mishima Aino Mausoleum] - Imperial Household Agency * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Rankings of Kofun areas (Sakai City)] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|太田茶臼山古墳|version=103903640|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Ibaraki, Osaka]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[sn:Ōta Chausuyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Ōta Chausuyama Kofun]] [[ja:太田茶臼山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Emperor Keitai]] [[Category:History of Ibaraki, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Oda Chausuyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11444409}}

Production of these haniwa has been linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Production Site|ja|史跡新池ハニワ工場公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinike Haniwa Production Site|qq=}} in Takatsuki.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第7巻 史料編 考古|2014|pp=293-297}} Other small tombs, believed to be satellite mounds, are located nearby. == Burial Individual == The actual burial individual is unknown, but the Imperial Household Agency attributes the tumulus to Emperor Keitai. Some scholars argue that [[:en:Imashirozuka Kofun|Imashirozuka Kofun]] (Takatsuki, Osaka Prefecture) better matches historical records and Emperor Keitai's era.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第1巻 通史|2012|pp=381-389}} == Excavated Items == burial goods such as cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, and roof tiles have been unearthed. These findings are stored and displayed at the {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Municipal Museum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Cultural Importance == The surrounding moat is Historic Site1 as part of a national historic site, and the tumulus holds significant cultural value as a testament to the burial practices and social structures of 5th-century Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Municipal Museum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/026/index.html Mishima Aino Mausoleum] - Imperial Household Agency * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Rankings of Kofun areas (Sakai City)] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|太田茶臼山古墳|version=103903640|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Ibaraki, Osaka]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[sn:Ōta Chausuyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Ōta Chausuyama Kofun]] [[ja:太田茶臼山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Emperor Keitai]] [[Category:History of Ibaraki, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Oda Chausuyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11444409}}

Kofun | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-south-southeast | map_dot_label = top | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | commemorates = {{ill|Keitai|WD=Q349277}} }} '''Oda Chausuyama Kofun''' (太田茶臼山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Ibaraki, Osaka|Ibaraki, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture, Oda. It is the largest tumulus in northern Osaka and Ranks as the 21st largest in Japan. Though the actual interred individual is unknown, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as "Mishima Aino Mausoleum," attributed to [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]]. == Overview == The tumulus was constructed on the {{ill|Tonda Plateau|ja|富田台地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tonda Plateau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} near the right bank of the [[:en:Yodo River|Yodo River]]. Current management falls under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, which recognizes it as Emperor Keitai's mausoleum. Excavations outside the surrounding moat have been conducted by the Osaka Prefectural and Ibaraki City Boards of Education in 1972 and 1988. The Imperial Household Agency's Sho-Ryo Division also carried out a survey at the mound's base in 2002. The tumulus features a keyhole type, with the front mound oriented south. It measures 226 meters in total length, making it the largest in northern Osaka. Its moat and embankments encircle the mound in a shield type, with figurative and cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] excavated from its surroundings. Production of these haniwa has been linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Production Site|ja|史跡新池ハニワ工場公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinike Haniwa Production Site|qq=}} in Takatsuki.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第7巻 史料編 考古|2014|pp=293-297}} Other small tombs, believed to be satellite mounds, are located nearby. == Burial Individual == The actual burial individual is unknown, but the Imperial Household Agency attributes the tumulus to Emperor Keitai. Some scholars argue that [[:en:Imashirozuka Kofun|Imashirozuka Kofun]] (Takatsuki, Osaka Prefecture) better matches historical records and Emperor Keitai's era.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第1巻 通史|2012|pp=381-389}} == Excavated Items == burial goods such as cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, and roof tiles have been unearthed. These findings are stored and displayed at the {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Cultural Importance == The surrounding moat is Historic Site1 as part of a national historic site, and the tumulus holds significant cultural value as a testament to the burial practices and social structures of 5th-century Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/026/index.html Mishima Aino Mausoleum] - Imperial Household Agency * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Rankings of Kofun areas (Sakai City)] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|太田茶臼山古墳|version=103903640|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Ibaraki, Osaka]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[sn:Ōta Chausuyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Ōta Chausuyama Kofun]] [[ja:太田茶臼山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Emperor Keitai]] [[Category:History of Ibaraki, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Oda Chausuyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11444409}}

This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Oda Chausuyama Kofun | image = File:Oda Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg | part_of = {{ill|Mishima Kofun Cluster|ja|三島古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima Kofun Cluster|qq=}} | location = {{ill|Ibaraki|WD=Q502403}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.844578|N|135.578433|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | area = Total length: 226 meters; height (front mound): 19.8 meters | built = Mid-5th century | Interred_individual = ([[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site) [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]] | excavations = Cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, and more | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Mishima Aino Mausoleum" (三嶋藍野陵) | notes = 21st largest tumulus in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Rankings of Kofun areas (National Version)] (Sakai City Website, updated May 13, 2018).
Similar in type to [[:en:Ichinoyama Kofun|Ichinoyama Kofun]] (Fujidera, Osaka Prefecture) | map = Japan Osaka | map_dot_label = Oda Chausuyama
Kofun | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-south-southeast | map_dot_label = top | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | commemorates = {{ill|Keitai|WD=Q349277}} }} '''Oda Chausuyama Kofun''' (太田茶臼山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Ibaraki, Osaka|Ibaraki, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture, Oda. It is the largest tumulus in northern Osaka and Ranks as the 21st largest in Japan. Though the actual interred individual is unknown, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as "Mishima Aino Mausoleum," attributed to [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]]. == Overview == The tumulus was constructed on the {{ill|Tonda Plateau|ja|富田台地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tonda Plateau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} near the right bank of the [[:en:Yodo River|Yodo River]]. Current management falls under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, which recognizes it as Emperor Keitai's mausoleum. Excavations outside the surrounding moat have been conducted by the Osaka Prefectural and Ibaraki City Boards of Education in 1972 and 1988. The Imperial Household Agency's Sho-Ryo Division also carried out a survey at the mound's base in 2002. The tumulus features a keyhole type, with the front mound oriented south. It measures 226 meters in total length, making it the largest in northern Osaka. Its moat and embankments encircle the mound in a shield type, with figurative and cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] excavated from its surroundings. Production of these haniwa has been linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Production Site|ja|史跡新池ハニワ工場公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinike Haniwa Production Site|qq=}} in Takatsuki.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第7巻 史料編 考古|2014|pp=293-297}} Other small tombs, believed to be satellite mounds, are located nearby. == Burial Individual == The actual burial individual is unknown, but the Imperial Household Agency attributes the tumulus to Emperor Keitai. Some scholars argue that [[:en:Imashirozuka Kofun|Imashirozuka Kofun]] (Takatsuki, Osaka Prefecture) better matches historical records and Emperor Keitai's era.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第1巻 通史|2012|pp=381-389}} == Excavated Items == burial goods such as cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, and roof tiles have been unearthed. These findings are stored and displayed at the {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Cultural Importance == The surrounding moat is Historic Site1 as part of a national historic site, and the tumulus holds significant cultural value as a testament to the burial practices and social structures of 5th-century Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/026/index.html Mishima Aino Mausoleum] - Imperial Household Agency * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Rankings of Kofun areas (Sakai City)] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|太田茶臼山古墳|version=103903640|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Ibaraki, Osaka]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[sn:Ōta Chausuyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Ōta Chausuyama Kofun]] [[ja:太田茶臼山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Emperor Keitai]] [[Category:History of Ibaraki, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Oda Chausuyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11444409}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

These findings are stored and displayed at the {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Cultural Importance == The surrounding moat is Historic Site1 as part of a national historic site, and the tumulus holds significant cultural value as a testament to the burial practices and social structures of 5th-century Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/026/index.html Mishima Aino Mausoleum] - Imperial Household Agency * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Rankings of Kofun areas (Sakai City)] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|太田茶臼山古墳|version=103903640|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Ibaraki, Osaka]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[sn:Ōta Chausuyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Ōta Chausuyama Kofun]] [[ja:太田茶臼山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Emperor Keitai]] [[Category:History of Ibaraki, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Oda Chausuyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11444409}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Oda Chausuyama Kofun]][[ja:太田茶臼山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11444409]] Wikidata name: Ōda Chausuyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Oda Chausuyama Kofun | image = File:Oda Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg | part_of = {{ill|Mishima Kofun Cluster|ja|三島古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima Kofun Cluster|qq=}} | location = {{ill|Ibaraki|WD=Q502403}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.844578|N|135.578433|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | area = Total length: 226 meters; height (front mound): 19.8 meters | built = Mid-5th century | Interred_individual = ([[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site) [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]] | excavations = Cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, and more | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Mishima Aino Mausoleum" (三嶋藍野陵) | notes = 21st largest tumulus in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Rankings of Kofun areas (National Version)] (Sakai City Website, updated May 13, 2018).
Similar in type to [[:en:Ichinoyama Kofun|Ichinoyama Kofun]] (Fujidera, Osaka Prefecture) | map = Japan Osaka | map_dot_label = Oda Chausuyama
Kofun | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-south-southeast | map_dot_label = top | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | commemorates = {{ill|Keitai|WD=Q349277}} }} '''Oda Chausuyama Kofun''' (太田茶臼山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Ibaraki, Osaka|Ibaraki, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture, Oda. It is the largest tumulus in northern Osaka and Ranks as the 21st largest in Japan. Though the actual interred individual is unknown, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as "Mishima Aino Mausoleum," attributed to [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]]. == Overview == The tumulus was constructed on the {{ill|Tonda Plateau|ja|富田台地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tonda Plateau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} near the right bank of the [[:en:Yodo River|Yodo River]]. Current management falls under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, which recognizes it as Emperor Keitai's mausoleum. Excavations outside the surrounding moat have been conducted by the Osaka Prefectural and Ibaraki City Boards of Education in 1972 and 1988. The Imperial Household Agency's Sho-Ryo Division also carried out a survey at the mound's base in 2002. The tumulus features a keyhole type, with the front mound oriented south. It measures 226 meters in total length, making it the largest in northern Osaka. Its moat and embankments encircle the mound in a shield type, with figurative and cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] excavated from its surroundings. Production of these haniwa has been linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Production Site|ja|史跡新池ハニワ工場公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinike Haniwa Production Site|qq=}} in Takatsuki.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第7巻 史料編 考古|2014|pp=293-297}} Other small tombs, believed to be satellite mounds, are located nearby. == Burial Individual == The actual burial individual is unknown, but the Imperial Household Agency attributes the tumulus to Emperor Keitai. Some scholars argue that [[:en:Imashirozuka Kofun|Imashirozuka Kofun]] (Takatsuki, Osaka Prefecture) better matches historical records and Emperor Keitai's era.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第1巻 通史|2012|pp=381-389}} == Excavated Items == burial goods such as cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, and roof tiles have been unearthed. These findings are stored and displayed at the {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Cultural Importance == The surrounding moat is Historic Site1 as part of a national historic site, and the tumulus holds significant cultural value as a testament to the burial practices and social structures of 5th-century Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/026/index.html Mishima Aino Mausoleum] - Imperial Household Agency * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Rankings of Kofun areas (Sakai City)] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|太田茶臼山古墳|version=103903640|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Ibaraki, Osaka]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[sn:Ōta Chausuyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Ōta Chausuyama Kofun]] [[ja:太田茶臼山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Emperor Keitai]] [[Category:History of Ibaraki, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Oda Chausuyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11444409}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Oda Chausuyama Kofun | image = File:Oda Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg | part_of = {{ill|Mishima Kofun Cluster|ja|三島古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima Kofun Cluster|qq=}} | location = {{ill|Ibaraki|WD=Q502403}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.844578|N|135.578433|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | area = Total length: 226 meters; height (front mound): 19.8 meters | built = Mid-5th century | Interred_individual = ([[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site) [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]] | excavations = Cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, and more | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Mishima Aino Mausoleum" (三嶋藍野陵) | notes = 21st largest tumulus in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Rankings of Kofun areas (National Version)] (Sakai City Website, updated May 13, 2018).
Similar in type to [[:en:Ichinoyama Kofun|Ichinoyama Kofun]] (Fujidera, Osaka Prefecture) | map = Japan Osaka | map_dot_label = Oda Chausuyama
Kofun | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-south-southeast | map_dot_label = top | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | commemorates = {{ill|Keitai|WD=Q349277}} }} '''Oda Chausuyama Kofun''' (太田茶臼山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Ibaraki, Osaka|Ibaraki, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture, Oda. It is the largest tumulus in northern Osaka and Ranks as the 21st largest in Japan. Though the actual interred individual is unknown, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as "Mishima Aino Mausoleum," attributed to [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]]. == Overview == The tumulus was constructed on the {{ill|Tonda Plateau|ja|富田台地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tonda Plateau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} near the right bank of the [[:en:Yodo River|Yodo River]]. Current management falls under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, which recognizes it as Emperor Keitai's mausoleum. Excavations outside the surrounding moat have been conducted by the Osaka Prefectural and Ibaraki City Boards of Education in 1972 and 1988. The Imperial Household Agency's Sho-Ryo Division also carried out a survey at the mound's base in 2002. The tumulus features a keyhole type, with the front mound oriented south. It measures 226 meters in total length, making it the largest in northern Osaka. Its moat and embankments encircle the mound in a shield type, with figurative and cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] excavated from its surroundings. Production of these haniwa has been linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Production Site|ja|史跡新池ハニワ工場公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinike Haniwa Production Site|qq=}} in Takatsuki.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第7巻 史料編 考古|2014|pp=293-297}} Other small tombs, believed to be satellite mounds, are located nearby. == Burial Individual == The actual burial individual is unknown, but the Imperial Household Agency attributes the tumulus to Emperor Keitai. Some scholars argue that [[:en:Imashirozuka Kofun|Imashirozuka Kofun]] (Takatsuki, Osaka Prefecture) better matches historical records and Emperor Keitai's era.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第1巻 通史|2012|pp=381-389}} == Excavated Items == burial goods such as cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, and roof tiles have been unearthed. These findings are stored and displayed at the {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Cultural Importance == The surrounding moat is Historic Site1 as part of a national historic site, and the tumulus holds significant cultural value as a testament to the burial practices and social structures of 5th-century Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/026/index.html Mishima Aino Mausoleum] - Imperial Household Agency * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Rankings of Kofun areas (Sakai City)] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|太田茶臼山古墳|version=103903640|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Ibaraki, Osaka]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[sn:Ōta Chausuyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Ōta Chausuyama Kofun]] [[ja:太田茶臼山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Emperor Keitai]] [[Category:History of Ibaraki, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Oda Chausuyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11444409}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11444409}}

These findings are stored and displayed at the {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Cultural Importance == The surrounding moat is Historic Site1 as part of a national historic site, and the tumulus holds significant cultural value as a testament to the burial practices and social structures of 5th-century Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/026/index.html Mishima Aino Mausoleum] - Imperial Household Agency * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Rankings of Kofun areas (Sakai City)] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|太田茶臼山古墳|version=103903640|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Ibaraki, Osaka]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[sn:Ōta Chausuyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Ōta Chausuyama Kofun]] [[ja:太田茶臼山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Emperor Keitai]] [[Category:History of Ibaraki, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Oda Chausuyama Kofun}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11444409}}

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Oda Chausuyama Kofun | image = File:Oda Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg | part_of = {{ill|Mishima Kofun Cluster|ja|三島古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima Kofun Cluster|qq=}} | location = {{ill|Ibaraki|WD=Q502403}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.844578|N|135.578433|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | area = Total length: 226 meters; height (front mound): 19.8 meters | built = Mid-5th century | Interred_individual = ([[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site) [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]] | excavations = Cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, and more | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Mishima Aino Mausoleum" (三嶋藍野陵) | notes = 21st largest tumulus in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Rankings of Kofun areas (National Version)] (Sakai City Website, updated May 13, 2018).
Similar in type to [[:en:Ichinoyama Kofun|Ichinoyama Kofun]] (Fujidera, Osaka Prefecture) | map = Japan Osaka | map_dot_label = Oda Chausuyama
Kofun | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-south-southeast | map_dot_label = top | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | commemorates = {{ill|Keitai|WD=Q349277}} }} '''Oda Chausuyama Kofun''' (太田茶臼山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Ibaraki, Osaka|Ibaraki, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture, Oda. It is the largest tumulus in northern Osaka and Ranks as the 21st largest in Japan. Though the actual interred individual is unknown, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as "Mishima Aino Mausoleum," attributed to [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]]. == Overview == The tumulus was constructed on the {{ill|Tonda Plateau|ja|富田台地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tonda Plateau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} near the right bank of the [[:en:Yodo River|Yodo River]]. Current management falls under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, which recognizes it as Emperor Keitai's mausoleum. Excavations outside the surrounding moat have been conducted by the Osaka Prefectural and Ibaraki City Boards of Education in 1972 and 1988. The Imperial Household Agency's Sho-Ryo Division also carried out a survey at the mound's base in 2002. The tumulus features a keyhole type, with the front mound oriented south. It measures 226 meters in total length, making it the largest in northern Osaka. Its moat and embankments encircle the mound in a shield type, with figurative and cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] excavated from its surroundings. Production of these haniwa has been linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Production Site|ja|史跡新池ハニワ工場公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinike Haniwa Production Site|qq=}} in Takatsuki.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第7巻 史料編 考古|2014|pp=293-297}} Other small tombs, believed to be satellite mounds, are located nearby. == Burial Individual == The actual burial individual is unknown, but the Imperial Household Agency attributes the tumulus to Emperor Keitai. Some scholars argue that [[:en:Imashirozuka Kofun|Imashirozuka Kofun]] (Takatsuki, Osaka Prefecture) better matches historical records and Emperor Keitai's era.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第1巻 通史|2012|pp=381-389}} == Excavated Items == burial goods such as cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, and roof tiles have been unearthed. These findings are stored and displayed at the {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Cultural Importance == The surrounding moat is Historic Site1 as part of a national historic site, and the tumulus holds significant cultural value as a testament to the burial practices and social structures of 5th-century Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/026/index.html Mishima Aino Mausoleum] - Imperial Household Agency * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Rankings of Kofun areas (Sakai City)] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|太田茶臼山古墳|version=103903640|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Ibaraki, Osaka]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[sn:Ōta Chausuyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Ōta Chausuyama Kofun]] [[ja:太田茶臼山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Emperor Keitai]] [[Category:History of Ibaraki, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Oda Chausuyama Kofun}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11444409}}

This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Oda Chausuyama Kofun | image = File:Oda Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg | part_of = {{ill|Mishima Kofun Cluster|ja|三島古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima Kofun Cluster|qq=}} | location = {{ill|Ibaraki|WD=Q502403}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.844578|N|135.578433|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | area = Total length: 226 meters; height (front mound): 19.8 meters | built = Mid-5th century | Interred_individual = ([[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site) [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]] | excavations = Cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, and more | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Mishima Aino Mausoleum" (三嶋藍野陵) | notes = 21st largest tumulus in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Rankings of Kofun areas (National Version)] (Sakai City Website, updated May 13, 2018).
Similar in type to [[:en:Ichinoyama Kofun|Ichinoyama Kofun]] (Fujidera, Osaka Prefecture) | map = Japan Osaka | map_dot_label = Oda Chausuyama
Kofun | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-south-southeast | map_dot_label = top | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | commemorates = {{ill|Keitai|WD=Q349277}} }} '''Oda Chausuyama Kofun''' (太田茶臼山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Ibaraki, Osaka|Ibaraki, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture, Oda. It is the largest tumulus in northern Osaka and Ranks as the 21st largest in Japan. Though the actual interred individual is unknown, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as "Mishima Aino Mausoleum," attributed to [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]]. == Overview == The tumulus was constructed on the {{ill|Tonda Plateau|ja|富田台地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tonda Plateau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} near the right bank of the [[:en:Yodo River|Yodo River]]. Current management falls under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, which recognizes it as Emperor Keitai's mausoleum. Excavations outside the surrounding moat have been conducted by the Osaka Prefectural and Ibaraki City Boards of Education in 1972 and 1988. The Imperial Household Agency's Sho-Ryo Division also carried out a survey at the mound's base in 2002. The tumulus features a keyhole type, with the front mound oriented south. It measures 226 meters in total length, making it the largest in northern Osaka. Its moat and embankments encircle the mound in a shield type, with figurative and cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] excavated from its surroundings. Production of these haniwa has been linked to the {{ill|Shinike Haniwa Production Site|ja|史跡新池ハニワ工場公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinike Haniwa Production Site|qq=}} in Takatsuki.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第7巻 史料編 考古|2014|pp=293-297}} Other small tombs, believed to be satellite mounds, are located nearby. == Burial Individual == The actual burial individual is unknown, but the Imperial Household Agency attributes the tumulus to Emperor Keitai. Some scholars argue that [[:en:Imashirozuka Kofun|Imashirozuka Kofun]] (Takatsuki, Osaka Prefecture) better matches historical records and Emperor Keitai's era.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第1巻 通史|2012|pp=381-389}} == Excavated Items == burial goods such as cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, and roof tiles have been unearthed. These findings are stored and displayed at the {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Cultural Importance == The surrounding moat is Historic Site1 as part of a national historic site, and the tumulus holds significant cultural value as a testament to the burial practices and social structures of 5th-century Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/026/index.html Mishima Aino Mausoleum] - Imperial Household Agency * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Rankings of Kofun areas (Sakai City)] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|太田茶臼山古墳|version=103903640|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Ōta Chausuyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Ōta Chausuyama Kofun]] [[ja:太田茶臼山古墳]] {{Commons category|Oda Chausuyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11444409}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Emperor Keitai]] [[Category:History of Ibaraki, Osaka]] [[Category:Ibaraki, Osaka]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

Some scholars argue that [[:en:Imashirozuka Kofun|Imashirozuka Kofun]] (Takatsuki, Osaka Prefecture) better matches historical records and Emperor Keitai's era.{{Harvid|新修茨木市史 第1巻 通史|2012|pp=381-389}} == Excavated Items == burial goods such as cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, and roof tiles have been unearthed. These findings are stored and displayed at the {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Cultural Importance == The surrounding moat is Historic Site1 as part of a national historic site, and the tumulus holds significant cultural value as a testament to the burial practices and social structures of 5th-century Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja|茨木市立文化財資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Municipal Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/026/index.html Mishima Aino Mausoleum] - Imperial Household Agency * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Rankings of Kofun areas (Sakai City)] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|太田茶臼山古墳|version=103903640|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Ōta Chausuyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Ōta Chausuyama Kofun]] [[ja:太田茶臼山古墳]] {{Commons category|Oda Chausuyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11444409}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Emperor Keitai]] [[Category:History of Ibaraki, Osaka]] [[Category:Ibaraki, Osaka]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]
